mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and signals easier financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is trending down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after downtrends in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips and rising FEDFUNDS periods systematically outperform easing periods over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and demand, which should depress risk appetite and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average 20-day return after such triggers is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A broad acceleration in retail sales and housing starts together signals a synchronized demand upswing that supports near-term earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform single-series effects, or if triggered months produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial output and corporate revenue momentum, which should pressure cyclical earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling INDPRO is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the rank correlation between INDPRO changes and forward returns is persistently above zero in any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5 percent, high policy restraint tightens valuation multiples and increases recession risk, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in a majority of FEDFUNDS > 4.5% observations, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero over any contiguous 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDP and retail sales signals that final demand is weakening across the economy, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not produce lower 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the conditional hit rate for negative returns stays below 50% over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS print in a stable labor market indicates continuing payroll expansion and income support, which should help equities post positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE does not rise in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if payroll gains without higher unemployment fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional mean return is not above zero in at least two-thirds of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS stays low, real rates remain accommodative and the market should reprice toward easier financial conditions, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low FEDFUNDS with rising CPIAUCSL is not associated with positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value versus either proxy alone over any 4-year rolling window.
